articles.title: Introducing Cache Response Rules
articles.excerpt: Perhaps you’ve seen something that should sail out of cache get dragged back to the origin by a stray Set-Cookie or Cache-Control, headers that can be difficult to change on the origin itself. Cache Response Rules is the fix, applied at the right time.

articles.title: ARC zonal shift support for EKS Auto Mode and Karpenter
articles.excerpt: In this post, we walk through how zonal shift integrates with Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) and what happens when a shift is triggered. We also show how to enable it on both self-managed Karpenter and EKS Auto Mode (EKS Auto) clusters.

articles.title: Your AI agents are ready. Is your data?

articles.title: The case for a cooldown: Why Dependabot now waits before issuing version updates

articles.title: 5 new ways Red Hat helps partners maximize business value
articles.excerpt: At Red Hat, our goal for the ecosystem has always been simple: build a predictable, profitable partner program for our partners to scale their business. As always, we remain committed to the future of open source, and that means continuously investing in the partners who help us bring that future to life.

articles.title: Why single AI agents fail at scale: Building governed multi-agent networks
articles.excerpt: A secured agent that can't reach anything is just expensive autocomplete with a badge. In "Why prompt-level guardrails aren't enough," I walked through how Red Hat AI allows you to give each agent a cryptographic identity and lock down what it can touch.
